# Benchmark Coverage Checklist: Bilingual Voice Benchmark Demo

**Purpose**: Validate that the specification covers the requested benchmark, dataset, pipeline, metric, report, and exclusion requirements before planning.
**Created**: 2026-06-06
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK001 Are text-mode user stories and acceptance criteria defined for tool-required and no-tool examples? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK002 Are audio-mode user stories and acceptance criteria defined for transcript-only, one-pass tool calling, and cascaded tool calling? [Coverage, Spec §User Stories 2-4]
- [x] CHK003 Are benchmark review/reporting user stories defined separately from request-processing flows? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 5]
- [x] CHK004 Are all four required pipelines A-D explicitly named and scoped in requirements? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022-FR-025]

## Tool Schema And Validation

- [x] CHK005 Is the canonical model output envelope specified with required fields for tool and no-tool outputs? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Spec §FR-006-FR-009]
- [x] CHK006 Is the `units.convert` schema specified with tool name, numeric value, source unit, target unit, and supported unit set? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008-FR-012]
- [x] CHK007 Are validation rejection cases specified for unsupported tools, missing arguments, nonnumeric values, and unsupported units? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014-FR-015]
- [x] CHK008 Are invalid JSON, repair attempt, first-pass parsability, and repaired-output reporting requirements distinguishable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-016-FR-019]

## Dataset And Audio Generation

- [x] CHK009 Are synthetic dataset size, language balance, no-tool ratio, expected labels, and expected answers specified with measurable counts? [Completeness, Spec §FR-026-FR-029]
- [x] CHK010 Are dataset versioning, deterministic split policy, and split stratification dimensions specified? [Clarity, Spec §FR-033-FR-034]
- [x] CHK011 Are text and audio examples required to share stable IDs for aligned modality comparisons? [Consistency, Spec §FR-030-FR-032]
- [x] CHK012 Are TTS generation requirements specified beyond requiring a synthesized audio version, including generation metadata or reproducibility expectations? [Completeness, Spec §FR-031]

## Metrics And Evaluation

- [x] CHK013 Are ASR WER requirements specified for audio transcript evaluation and Russian/English coverage? [Completeness, Spec §FR-045, Spec §SC-004]
- [x] CHK014 Are tool-use metric requirements specified for parsability, decision accuracy, exact match, argument match, precision, recall, and false alarm rate? [Completeness, Spec §FR-037-FR-045]
- [x] CHK015 Is the treatment of partially correct tool calls specified for official exact-match metrics and failure diagnostics? [Clarity, Spec §FR-039-FR-042]
- [x] CHK016 Is the no-tool false alarm definition explicit and consistent with precision/recall reporting? [Clarity, Spec §FR-043-FR-044]
- [x] CHK017 Are modality-gap requirements specified using shared semantic IDs across text and audio outputs? [Consistency, Spec §FR-032, Spec §FR-046, Spec §SC-006]

## Report And Failure Analysis

- [x] CHK018 Are final report contents specified, including dataset summary, per-pipeline metrics, language splits, confusion matrix, WER, modality gap, best-pipeline rationale, and failure cases? [Completeness, Spec §FR-048, Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK019 Are failure categories specified for JSON parsing, schema validation, tool decision, tool name, numeric value, units, transcript, execution, and answer errors? [Coverage, Spec §Key Entities, Spec §Benchmark and Dataset Requirements]
- [x] CHK020 Are benchmark artifacts required to record raw outputs, parsed outputs, validation errors, repair attempts, optional execution results, final answers, and metrics? [Traceability, Spec §FR-047]

## Non-Goals And Exclusions

- [x] CHK021 Is weather explicitly excluded from MVP datasets, prompts, schemas, pipelines, tests, and reports? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Allowed Tools]
- [x] CHK022 Are optional endpoint scope and full natural-speech robustness positioned as secondary or later concerns? [Clarity, Spec §FR-051, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK023 Are heavy/full benchmark expectations distinguished from bounded validation expectations? [Clarity, Spec §Benchmark and Dataset Requirements]

## Notes

- All requested benchmark coverage areas are represented in the specification after the TTS metadata clarification.
